name: Management of Technology

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: This is a tight, well-themed certificate: MGMT 620 Management of Technology anchors it, and EM 636 Project Management, HRM 601 Organizational Behavior in Technology-Based Organizations, MGMT 650 Knowledge Management, and MIS 645/648 (IT competitive advantage and decision support) all cluster cleanly around leading technology organizations. The six-course pool is coherent and on-message with little dilution.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a technology-strategy or innovation-management course to complete the arc from managing technology operations to setting technology direction.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: missingCourseCount is 0 and deepestPrereqChain is 1 (MIS 648 after MIS 645), an appropriate, reference-clean structure for a focused certificate where courses should be largely independently accessible.
dimensions.suggestion: Consider recommending MGMT 620 as the entry course so students establish the technology-management frame before the electives.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No certificate learning outcomes are stated, so the specific technology-management competencies a completer gains are not published.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 3-4 outcomes such as leading technical teams, managing technology projects, and aligning IT with competitive strategy.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: The null statedTotalCredits is a transparency note appropriate to a certificate; six uniform 3-credit courses imply a standard choose-four / 12-credit credential, though the required count is not shown.
dimensions.suggestion: State the credit total and the number of courses required from the six-course menu.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: All six courses have clear, specific descriptions and consistent credits, but the entry lacks a total-credit statement, a selection rule, and outcomes, so it reads as a course list rather than a framed credential.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a brief purpose-and-credits header and indicate how many of the six courses are required.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The set is solidly relevant to technology management, with MGMT 620, MIS 645/648, and HRM 601 mapping to real roles, but the emphasis is somewhat classic (knowledge management, decision support) and lacks explicit analytics, agile, or emerging-technology content that defines current technology leadership.
dimensions.suggestion: Introduce a modern elective on digital innovation or technology analytics so the certificate reflects contemporary practice, not only established IT management.

descriptionQuality.justification: Descriptions are present and specific across all six courses (MGMT 620 on how new technologies transform industries and shape product evolution, MGMT 650 on frameworks for implementing knowledge-management efforts), with no thin or placeholder text.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites are unambiguous and dangling-free (missingCourseCount=0), with a single sensible gate (MIS 648 after MIS 645) and open access elsewhere, internally consistent for a certificate.

missingTopics.rationale: MGMT 620 covers managing technology broadly but no course focuses on setting technology direction, R&D portfolio choices, and roadmapping.
The certificate manages existing technology (project, knowledge, IT) but lacks an innovation/product-development course covering the creation of new technology offerings.
Beyond MIS 648's decision support there is no analytics course, leaving technology managers without hands-on data-driven decision skills.
EM 636 covers traditional project management but not agile/DevOps or digital product management, now standard in technology organizations.
No course addresses security and risk governance, a core responsibility of technology leaders that the certificate omits.

emergingTrends.rationale: MGMT 620 and MGMT 650 could address how organizations adopt and govern generative AI, the defining current technology-management challenge.
MIS 645 (IT and competitive advantage) is well placed to extend toward platform business models and enterprise digital transformation.
Modernizing EM 636 toward agile delivery would align the project-management content with how technology work is actually run.
Updating MIS 648 with contemporary analytics and AI-assisted decision support keeps the certificate's decision-making core current.
Security and resilience are now board-level concerns technology managers must lead, warranting a dedicated elective.

topSuggestions: Add a technology-strategy/roadmapping or innovation-management course to complete the arc from operating to directing technology.
State the certificate's total credits and how many of the six courses are required.
Publish 3-4 learning outcomes describing the technology-management competencies delivered.
Introduce a modern digital-innovation or technology-analytics elective to raise the certificate's currency.
Modernize EM 636 toward agile and MIS 648 toward AI-assisted decision support.
